title: Is It Too Late To Consider Matson (MATX) After Its Strong Multi Year Share Price Run?
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

If you are wondering whether Matson's share price around US$166 still offers value, the key question is how that price compares with the underlying business. The stock has returned 1.9% over the past week, 34.5% year to date and 46.2% over the last year. This comes even with a 1.3% decline over the past month, a 203.8% gain over three years and 146.7% over five years. Recent coverage has focused on Matson as a Shipping industry name that has seen strong multi year returns. This has naturally sharpened attention on whether the current price still makes sense. At the same time, investors are weighing sector wide factors such as freight demand, capacity trends and trade flows when thinking about where the stock sits today. Simply Wall St's valuation checks currently give Matson a 2 out of 6 valuation score. The next step is to look at how different methods like DCFs and multiples compare, and then consider an even richer way to think about value that will be covered at the end of this article.

Matson scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: Matson Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model projects a company’s future cash flows and then discounts those amounts back to today’s dollars to estimate what the business might be worth right now.

For Matson, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $219.8 million, with analyst style estimates provided for 2024 at $486.9 million. Beyond the near term, Simply Wall St extrapolates ten year Free Cash Flow projections, which sit in a range around $60 million by 2035 according to the supplied figures.

Rolling these projected cash flows into the DCF model results in an estimated intrinsic value of about $42.83 per share. Compared with the current share price around $166, the DCF output suggests Matson is very expensive on this cash flow basis, with the implied intrinsic discount indicating the stock is 288.5% overvalued.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Matson may be overvalued by 288.5%. Discover 62 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.MATX Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Matson.

Story Continues

Approach 2: Matson Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Matson, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of current earnings. It ties the share price directly to the business’s ability to generate profit today.

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how much growth investors expect in the future and how much risk they see in those earnings. Higher growth and lower perceived risk usually support a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk tend to justify a lower one.

Matson currently trades on a P/E of 11.38x. That is very close to the Shipping industry average of 11.39x and below the broader peer group average of 15.91x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Matson is 11.71x, which is an estimate of what the P/E might be given factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and specific risks. This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple peer or industry comparison because it is tailored to the company’s own profile rather than broad group averages.

Comparing the current P/E of 11.38x with the Fair Ratio of 11.71x suggests Matson’s valuation is about right on this earnings multiple.

Result: ABOUT RIGHTNYSE:MATX P/E Ratio as at Apr 2026

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 20 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Matson Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to think about valuation, and that is through Narratives, which let you attach a clear story about Matson to the numbers you believe in, such as fair value, future revenue, earnings and margins, so that the story, the forecast and the valuation all line up in one place.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives are simple tools on the Community page that help you compare your own view of Matson with others, see a Fair Value based on that view, and then line it up against the current share price to help you decide whether the gap between the two is big enough to consider buying or selling.

Narratives on the platform are refreshed when new information comes in, such as earnings or news, and you can see how different investors frame Matson, from a more optimistic stance with a Fair Value around US$213 or US$160, through to a more cautious view nearer US$115, then decide which version of the story feels closest to your own expectations.

For Matson, here are previews of two leading Matson Narratives to make comparison easier:

🐂 Matson Bull Case

Fair value: US$213

Implied discount to this fair value: 21.9% undervalued vs the last close around US$166

Expected revenue growth used in this view: 3.44% per year

Assumes steady revenue growth supported by diversified shipping routes, fleet modernization, and expanded logistics services. Relies on protected U.S. trade lanes and Jones Act exposure for pricing power and relatively stable earnings and cash flows. Accepts risks around global trade uncertainty, concentrated trade lanes, high vessel capex, and rising competition, but still treats the current price as below fair value.

🐻 Matson Bear Case

Fair value: US$115

Implied premium to this fair value: 44.7% overvalued vs the last close around US$166

Expected revenue trend used in this view: 0.14% annual revenue decline

Focuses on pressure from potential long term declines in Asia origin volumes, changing trade policies, and higher compliance and environmental costs. Highlights concentration in a few key trade lanes and exposure to regional slowdowns and stronger global logistics competitors. Builds in lower revenue growth and shrinking margins, which pulls fair value down to US$115 and frames the current price as high against that backdrop.

These two Narratives outline a wide fair value range from US$115 to US$213. The key for you is deciding which set of assumptions aligns more closely with how you view Matson’s routes, margins, and long term demand.

title: Advanced Energy, Matson, MasTec, FuelCell Energy, and Primoris Shares Are Soaring, What You Need To Know
content:
What Happened?

A number of stocks jumped in the morning session after markets rebounded, driven by stabilizing oil prices and reports that President Trump was considering an end to the military conflict in Iran.

According to The Wall Street Journal, the president communicated to aides his willingness to de-escalate military hostilities, even if the strategically important Strait of Hormuz remained partially closed. This news helped soothe investor concerns about a prolonged conflict and its potential to spike energy costs, which can impact industrial operations and consumer spending. The positive shift in sentiment was reflected across major indexes, with the S&P 500 jumping over 1% as oil prices retreated from their recent highs.

The stock market overreacts to news, and big price drops can present good opportunities to buy high-quality stocks.

Among others, the following stocks were impacted:

Electronic Components company Advanced Energy (NASDAQ:AEIS) jumped 3.1%. Is now the time to buy Advanced Energy? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Marine Transportation company Matson (NYSE:MATX) jumped 2.8%. Is now the time to buy Matson? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Engineering and Design Services company MasTec (NYSE:MTZ) jumped 2.7%. Is now the time to buy MasTec? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Renewable Energy company FuelCell Energy (NASDAQ:FCEL) jumped 3.1%. Is now the time to buy FuelCell Energy? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free. Construction and Maintenance Services company Primoris (NYSE:PRIM) jumped 3.9%. Is now the time to buy Primoris? Access our full analysis report here, it’s free.

Zooming In On Primoris (PRIM)

Primoris’s shares are quite volatile and have had 19 moves greater than 5% over the last year. In that context, today’s move indicates the market considers this news meaningful but not something that would fundamentally change its perception of the business.

The previous big move we wrote about was about 21 hours ago when the stock dropped 7.2% on the news that markets reacted to President Trump's threat to "completely obliterate" Iran's energy infrastructure and the critical Kharg Island hub. The ultimatum raised the specter of a total energy supply shock. Notably, Kharg Island handles 90% of Iran's crude exports. The escalating rhetoric, including potential ground force deployment to seize fuel hubs, drove a flight to safety.

Primoris is up 7.1% since the beginning of the year, but at $139.83 per share, it is still trading 17.4% below its 52-week high of $169.36 from February 2026. Investors who bought $1,000 worth of Primoris’s shares 5 years ago would now be looking at an investment worth $4,221.

Story Continues

title: Reflecting On Marine Transportation Stocks’ Q4 Earnings: Matson (NYSE:MATX)
content:
Looking back on marine transportation stocks’ Q4 earnings, we examine this quarter’s best and worst performers, including Matson (NYSE:MATX) and its peers.

The growth of e-commerce and global trade continues to drive demand for shipping services, presenting opportunities for marine transportation companies. While ocean freight is more fuel efficient and therefore cheaper than its air and ground counterparts, it results in slower delivery times, presenting a trade off. To improve transit speeds, the industry continues to invest in digitization to optimize fleets and routes. However, marine transportation companies are still at the whim of economic cycles. Consumer spending, for example, can greatly impact the demand for these companies’ offerings while fuel costs can influence profit margins. Geopolitical tensions can also affect access to trade routes, and if certain countries are banned from using passageways like the Panama Canal, costs can spiral out of control.

The 5 marine transportation stocks we track reported a strong Q4. As a group, revenues beat analysts’ consensus estimates by 0.9%.

While some marine transportation stocks have fared somewhat better than others, they have collectively declined. On average, share prices are down 3% since the latest earnings results.

Matson (NYSE:MATX)

Founded by a Swedish orphan, Matson (NYSE:MATX) is a provider of ocean transportation and logistics services.

Matson reported revenues of $851.9 million, down 4.3% year on year. This print exceeded analysts’ expectations by 0.5%. Overall, it was a very strong quarter for the company with a beat of analysts’ EPS and EBITDA estimates.

Matt Cox, Matson's Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, commented, "Matson had a solid finish to the year with consolidated fourth quarter results that exceeded our expectations. For the quarter, Ocean Transportation operating income approached the level achieved in the prior year period primarily due to higher than expected freight rates and volume in our China service driven by strong e-commerce and e-goods demand."Matson Total Revenue

Matson delivered the slowest revenue growth of the whole group. Unsurprisingly, the stock is down 4.8% since reporting and currently trades at $159.00.

Is now the time to buy Matson? Access our full analysis of the earnings results here, it’s free.

Best Q4: Scorpio Tankers (NYSE:STNG)

Operating one of the youngest fleets in the industry, Scorpio Tankers (NYSE: STNG) is an international provider of marine transportation services, specializing in the shipment of refined petroleum.

Story Continues

Scorpio Tankers reported revenues of $241.4 million, up 25.6% year on year, outperforming analysts’ expectations by 4.3%. The business had an exceptional quarter with an impressive beat of analysts’ EBITDA and revenue estimates.Scorpio Tankers Total Revenue

Scorpio Tankers delivered the biggest analyst estimates beat and fastest revenue growth among its peers. The market seems happy with the results as the stock is up 5.5% since reporting. It currently trades at $73.75.

Is now the time to buy Scorpio Tankers? Access our full analysis of the earnings results here, it’s free.

Weakest Q4: Pangaea (NASDAQ:PANL)

Established in 1996, Pangaea Logistics (NASDAQ:PANL) specializes in global logistics and transportation services, focusing on the shipment of dry bulk cargoes.

Pangaea reported revenues of $183.9 million, up 24.9% year on year, exceeding analysts’ expectations by 1.2%. Still, it was a softer quarter as it posted a significant miss of analysts’ EBITDA and EPS estimates.

As expected, the stock is down 17.5% since the results and currently trades at $6.89.

Read our full analysis of Pangaea’s results here.

Kirby (NYSE:KEX)

Transporting goods along all U.S. coasts, Kirby (NYSE:KEX) provides inland and coastal marine transportation services.

Kirby reported revenues of $851.8 million, up 6.2% year on year. This number lagged analysts' expectations by 1.4%. More broadly, it was a satisfactory quarter as it also produced a solid beat of analysts’ EBITDA estimates but a slight miss of analysts’ revenue estimates.

Kirby had the weakest performance against analyst estimates among its peers. The stock is up 3.5% since reporting and currently trades at $132.58.

Read our full, actionable report on Kirby here, it’s free.

Genco (NYSE:GNK)

Headquartered in NYC, Genco (NYSE:GNK) is a shipping company that transports dry bulk cargo along worldwide maritime routes.

Genco reported revenues of $77.17 million, up 16.8% year on year. This print was in line with analysts’ expectations. It was a strong quarter as it also recorded a solid beat of analysts’ EBITDA estimates.

The stock is down 1.7% since reporting and currently trades at $22.16.

Read our full, actionable report on Genco here, it’s free.

Market Update

Late in 2025 into early 2026, there was hand wringing around artificial intelligence. For software companies, the fear was that AI would erode pricing power and compress margins as new tools made it easier to replicate what once required expensive enterprise platforms. Crypto investors had their own version of the same anxiety: if AI agents could trade, allocate capital, and manage wallets autonomously, what exactly was the long-term value of today’s crypto infrastructure?

These concerns triggered a noticeable rotation away from these sectors and into safer havens. But markets rarely dwell on one narrative for long. Spring 2026 came, and the focus shifted abruptly from technological disruption to geopolitical risk. The US’ conflict with Iran became the dominant driver of market psychology, and when geopolitics takes center stage, the script changes quickly. Investors stop debating growth rates and start worrying about oil supply, inflation, and global stability.

Want to invest in winners with rock-solid fundamentals? Check out our Top 6 Stocks and add them to your watchlist. These companies are poised for growth regardless of the political or macroeconomic climate.

title: Matson Leadership Shift Puts Focus On Commercial Strategy And Valuation Concerns
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

Matson (NYSE:MATX) announced that Executive Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer John Lauer plans to retire. Chris Scott has been appointed to succeed Lauer as Chief Commercial Officer. The transition marks a change in leadership for the company’s commercial operations.

Matson, a U.S. focused ocean transportation and logistics company, relies heavily on commercial leadership to manage routes, customer relationships, and freight mix across its core services. Changes at the EVP and Chief Commercial Officer level can influence how the business prioritizes trade lanes, contract structures, and service offerings, which are key areas investors often watch in shipping and logistics.

For shareholders tracking NYSE:MATX, this handover puts attention on how commercial priorities are set and communicated over time. It can be useful to monitor how the company frames this transition in future earnings calls, investor presentations, and operational updates, especially around customer retention, network decisions, and capital allocation tied to commercial strategy.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Matson by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Matson.NYSE:MATX 1-Year Stock Price Chart

Does the team leading Matson have what it takes? See our full breakdown of the management team's track record and compensation.

Quick Assessment

✅ Price vs Analyst Target: At US$163.24 versus a US$213.00 analyst target, the share price sits about 23% below that reference point. ❌ Simply Wall St Valuation: Shares are described as trading at around 280.9% above estimated fair value, a very large premium to that model. ❌ Recent Momentum: The 30 day return of about 2.2% decline shows recent price pressure as this leadership change is announced.

There is only one way to know the right time to buy, sell or hold Matson. Head to Simply Wall St's company report for the latest analysis of Matson's Fair Value.

Key Considerations

📊 A new Chief Commercial Officer could influence route focus, contract mix and pricing, which are all closely tied to Matson's revenue quality story. 📊 Watch how volumes, net income margin of 13.3% and commercial commentary trend in upcoming results under Chris Scott. ⚠️ With earnings forecast to decline by an average of 3% a year and shares flagged as heavily above estimated fair value, execution risk around this transition matters.

Dig Deeper

For the full picture, including more risks and rewards, check out the complete Matson analysis. Alternatively, you can check out the community page for Matson to see how other investors believe this latest news will impact the company's narrative.
